cards.definition: The two windows in the year when leaf tissue analysis for nutrient status is ideally carried out.
cards.analogy: Two scheduled check-in points rather than a single annual one or a constant stream of testing.
cards.why_it_matters: A leaf sample taken outside these windows may not reflect the plant's typical nutrient status.
cards.cloze: Coffee leaf analysis is ideally done during ___.

cards.definition: Which pair of leaves, counted from the growing tip, should be collected for Arabica leaf nutrient analysis.
cards.analogy: A specific rung on the ladder, not just 'a leaf from the plant' — position affects what the tissue reading actually reflects.
cards.why_it_matters: Sampling the wrong leaf pair would skew nutrient readings even with correct lab technique.
cards.cloze: For Arabica leaf analysis, the ___ pair of leaves from the growing tip is collected.

cards.definition: Which pair of leaves, counted from the growing tip, should be collected for Robusta leaf nutrient analysis — a different position than Arabica's.
cards.analogy: A different rung on the same ladder than Arabica's sampling point, reflecting the two species' different growth habits.
cards.why_it_matters: Confirms Arabica and Robusta leaf-sampling protocols aren't interchangeable, even though the method looks similar.
cards.cloze: For Robusta leaf analysis, the ___ pair of leaves from the growing tip is collected.
